title: GoapActionDef
description: One plannable GOAP action (ch03, F.E.A.R. case study): symbolic preconditions and effects over the agent's predicate state (catalog condition names and belief keys; scalar values, missing booleans read as false), a cost formula (personality lives in cost — see CostProfileDef), and the execution binding the 3-state layer realizes (GoTo → Animate → effects).

description.description: One line for humans and LLMs (surfaced by the manifest).

inherits.description: Parent def ID (same kind); objects deep-merge, scalars override, arrays replace or take {"$append"/"$remove"} patches.

preconditions.description: Predicate map that must match the agent's state for this action to be applicable.

effects.description: Predicate map this action establishes (applied to beliefs on completion).

cost.description: Cost formula (agent needs/stats/conditions/flags in scope). Evaluated once per plan request.

moveTo.description: Where to go first, if anywhere: a MoveTo target spec ("enemy", "spawn", [x,y,z], ...).

animation.description: Animation played after arrival, if any.

animationBlocking.description: Non-interruptible animations block replanning while they play (ch05 §5.6).

items.description: A effect primitive payload; see the manifest for arg signatures.

runEffects.description: RPG effect primitives run on completion (source = agent; target = perceived enemy when known).
